raw_consumer_statement:
In XX/XX/XXXX, I stayed at XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX and experienced severe, documented failures that rendered the stay materially unfit for the price paid. The stay was so bad, I checked out early and stayed at a airport hotel. 

XX/XX/XXXX, I contacted XXXX and XXXX XXXX in writing to resolve my charges amicably. I never received a response. XX/XX/XXXX and again XX/XX/XXXX, I disputed the charge on my American Express XXXX card ( ending XXXX ) and provided extensive supporting evidence. Amex denied both disputes. I am filing this complaint against American Express for failing to uphold my chargeback rights under the Fair Credit Billing Act. 

What happened : During my stay, I experienced the following : Food quality was unacceptable Meals were consistently poor and did not reflect what was advertised or charged for at a premium all-inclusive resort. 

Restaurant reservations were inaccessible Despite the resort 's advertised dining options, reservations were effectively unavailable, denying me access to services included in my stay. 

HVAC failure My room had no functioning air conditioning in a tropical climate, making it uninhabitable. 
Excessive noise Persistent disruptive noise made rest impossible. 

Maintenance and safety hazards Physical conditions in the room posed safety concerns that were reported and not remedied. 

Management indifference Staff and management failed to address or resolve any of these issues despite repeated requests. 

These failures were so significant that I departed the resort early and relocated to an airport hotel at my own expense. 

What I submitted to Amex : I provided American Express with a formal written dispute letter detailing all XXXX grievances, supported by a separate evidence document that included corroborating third-party guest reviews independent accounts describing the same failures. This is not a subjective complaint ; the issues I experienced are well-documented across the resort 's public review history. 

Amex 's response : American Express denied my dispute without adequately weighing the evidence I submitted. The denial was not explained in meaningful detail. Under the Fair Credit Billing Act, I am entitled to a proper investigation and a chargeback when a merchant fails to deliver services as represented. Amex did not meet that standard here. 

What I am seeking : A full chargeback of the XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX charge from XX/XX/XXXX. I am requesting that the CFPB require American Express to reopen and properly investigate this dispute in light of the documented evidence provided.
